strategy: single chunk (file too short to split)

pages.text:
Case study: training at home
What if a client trains at home and only has access to barbells and dumbbells? Exercise
selection can then change as follows.

Chest: Dumbbells will have to be used for the fly. Bands and chains would be great here. If
someone doesn't have them and their shoulders act up during DB flies, I like to use fly-presses:
it's basically a fly movement at the top that turns into a press in the bottom position.

Lateral delt & upper traps: DB laterals with a lot of momentum while keeping tension on
the muscle

Rear delts: High DB rows and reverse DB flies with a lot of momentum while keeping tension
on the muscle

Lats: Stick to bodyweight exercises and don't resort to pull-overs.

Biceps: Barbell (drag) curl, Scott DB curl

Quads: If you have bands, you can do terminal knee extensions as isolation work.

Hams, glutes, calves, triceps & core: No major limitations here.

full_text:
--- Page 1 ---
Case study: training at home
What if a client trains at home and only has access to barbells and dumbbells? Exercise
selection can then change as follows.

Chest: Dumbbells will have to be used for the fly. Bands and chains would be great here. If
someone doesn't have them and their shoulders act up during DB flies, I like to use fly-presses:
it's basically a fly movement at the top that turns into a press in the bottom position.

Lateral delt & upper traps: DB laterals with a lot of momentum while keeping tension on
the muscle

Rear delts: High DB rows and reverse DB flies with a lot of momentum while keeping tension
on the muscle

Lats: Stick to bodyweight exercises and don't resort to pull-overs.

Biceps: Barbell (drag) curl, Scott DB curl

Quads: If you have bands, you can do terminal knee extensions as isolation work.

Hams, glutes, calves, triceps & core: No major limitations here.
